entry.title: Create New Catalog, Space, Page, Section and Role – S4HANA Private Cloud
entry.content:
Like my previous article on classic concept of Fiori Groups in combination with Catalogs, this article is based on single-functional consultant approach where FICO consultants don't have to depend on ABAP or BASIS consultants to achieve overall objective.

We are going to develop a unified Fiori Space, create its relevant page, add related section therein, and then finally assign the respective Fiori tiles / apps.

For smoother learning and easier follow-up, I have devided this article in five main areas:

A. Create New Transport Request 

B. Create New Z Catalog and map it with required Fiori Apps

C. Create New Role and Assign the New Catalog

D. Create New Space, New Page and New Section

E. Assign Fiori Apps to Space, Pages and Sections

So lets start;

A. Create New Transport Request

Go to t-code SE10 and create a new TR where all configuration activities will be saved.

Click  button

Chose the radio button “Customizing Request”

New Transport Request will be created.

B. Create New Z Catalog and map it with required Fiori Apps

  1. In GUI, enter t-code /n/UI2/FLPD_CUST

Following window will open in the browser, this is SAP Fiori Launchpad Designer.

  1. We will create catalog for GL Report F2217 (Display Line Items in General Ledger)

Go to SAP online Fiori Library https://fioriappslibrary.hana.ondemand.com/sap/fix/externalViewer/#

and find the app F2217

Go to Tab  and expand the node ‘Configuration’

Find the catalog (either technical or business),

I chose the technical catalog SAP_TC_FIN_ACC_COMMON

  1. Go back to FLP Designer page (shown in step B-1).

Click on button available on right top corner of FLP Designer screen.

Following pop-up window will appear, here select the Transport Request you created in step A.

Click OK button to close the pop-up window. Now all activities will save in this transport request.

  1. Click on button available on left bottom of the screen.

A pop-up window will appear, here provide the Title and ID of new catalog.

Click on save button to create our new catalog.

  1. Now in catalog search field, specify the standard technical catalog SAP_TC_FIN_ACC_COMMON

Here find the app F2217

Click on the button “Tiles” as shown below:

Tile details will appear in table as below:

Select the row and you will notice few buttons have been enabled in bottom tray.

One of these isclick it, and following pop-up window will show:

Here provide the new catalog ID which we created in step B-4,

Select the catalog and SAP will save the reference of desired tile F2217 in our new catalog.

  1. Now on same screen, select the button “Target Mapping”

Here select the appropriate Semantic Object “GLAccount” and Action “displayGLLineItemReportingView” as shown below:

Now click on buttonand following pop-up window will appear:

Here provide the new catalog ID which we created in step B-4,

Select the catalog and SAP will save the reference of Target Mapping in our new catalog.

Now specify the new Catalog ID in search field (as shown below):

Click on Tile and Target Mapping button to confirm the assignment of same semantic object and action.

This confirms the Tile mapping and Target mapping.

We have completed the creation of new Catalog and it’s mapping with the required app F2217.

C. Create New Role and Assign the New Catalog

  1. Go to t-code PFCG and create new role ZFI_GL_REPORTS by clicking the button

     

  2. Click on tab Select Transaction -> SAP Fiori Launchpad -> Launchpad Catalog

     

Following pop-up window will appear,

Here specify the created new catalog ZFI_GL_REPORTS to assign with the role.

Save the data and notice the new catalog assignment.

This completes the catalog assignment to the role.

D. Create New Space, New Page and New Section

  1. Create New Space

On Fiori, access the app “Manage Launchpad Spaces”

Click on button

Following pop-up window will appear, here enter the Space ID, Short description, Space title and specify the TR where space data will be saved.

Click onbutton

On following screen, as shown below, click on button.

Space is now created and saved.

  1. Create New Page

Access the Fiori app “Manage Launchpad Pages”

Following screen will show:

Here click on button.

Following pop-up window will appear, here enter the Page ID, Short description, Page title and specify the TR where space data will be saved.

Click onbutton to create the Page.

Following screen will show:

  1. Create New Section

On Page screen, click on  button.

Following field will appear:

Specify the Section title

Click on button to save the section.

E. Assign Fiori Apps to Space, Pages and Sections

  1. In GUI, go to t-code PFCG and select the role ZFI_GL_REPORTS, we already created in step C-1.

Click on button

Go to tab, and access the path as shown below:

On following pop-up window, assign the Space ZFI_GL_REPORTS

Click on  button

Now click on save button and Space will be assigned to the role.

  1. Assign Page to Space

In Fiori app “Manage Launchpad Spaces”, select the already created space ZFI_GL_REPORTS

Now click on  button

List of available pages will show.

Here find our page ZFI_GL_REPORTS

Select this page by clicking checkbox and then click on button

Click on button.

Page is now assigned to Space.

  1. Assign Fiori App / Tile to Section

Access the Fiori App “Manage Launchpad Pages”

Select the page ZFI_GL_REPORTS

Click on  button

Following screen will appear:

Select the Fiori app “Display Line Items in General Ledger”

and click on  button

Now this Fiori tile will show as assigned to page (as shown below)

Click on  button.

This completes the Fiori tile assignment to Page and section.

Now the required app is visible for user on Fiori interface.

Now you can see the tile under the Space and Page.

Just go to User Homepage and find the Space “GL Reports”

Here you can see the Page and Tile under the same Space.

This concludes the article to buidup the Space, Page, Section and Tile/App assignment.

Hope you have enjoyed the read.

Please do comment for any observations and recommendations.

Best Regards,

entry.title: Re: ABAP Versions Explorer
entry.content: Added: Per-instance cache for rendered diff HTML. Key: object type + name + versno old/new + flags (blame, two_pane, compact, debug). On hit — returns stored HTML immediately, skipping source load, diff and blame computation. On miss — computes as usual and stores the result. Cache is invalidated automatically when the popup window closes.

entry.title: Re: Asset data migration inconsistent
entry.content:
Hi 

 

The discrepancy you are seeing moving from a flat 2  (9,975.96 EUR) to 2.05  (10,207.95 EUR) is a classic behavior in SAP S/4HANA Asset Accounting during mid-year migrations. The system is likely not ignoring your 2 %  configuration rather, it is performing a Catch Up or Smoothing calculation based on the remaining net book value and the remaining useful life.

The Remaining Life Calculation Logic In S/4HANA, when you migrate an asset mid-year, the system validates the Net Book Value  against the Remaining Useful Life. Total Life: 50 Years. Expired Life at Transfer 28 Years, 6 Months. Remaining Useful Life: 21 Years, 6 Months or 21.5 years. If the system is set to calculate depreciation based on the remaining life and the remaining value, the math looks like this

Annual Depreciation = NBV at Transfer\Remaining Life

Annual Depreciation = 219,471.01  - 4,987.98  \ 21.0

However, if we look at your specific calculation  219,471.01 \ 21.5  = 10,207.95

This matches your "incorrect" value exactly. The system is taking the NBV as of the start of the year and dividing it by the remaining life as of that same date to ensure the asset fully depreciates to zero by the end of the 50-year term.

The National Code 2015 2% is likely a Straight Line from APC key. However, when you migrated the asset, the accumulated depreciation you entered 279,326.89 might not perfectly align with a mathematical 2% per year since 1998.

If there is even a small rounding difference in the legacy accumulated depreciation, SAP corrects the rate for the remaining periods to ensure the asset reaches a zero book value exactly at the end of year 50.

To bring this back to the expected 9,975.96 check  in Transaction AFAMP Change Period Control Methods and AFAMA Depreciation Keys, Depreciation Calculation Method, Check if the Multi-Level Method is set to calculate based on Remaining Life instead of Installation Date.  If it is set to Remaining Life, SAP will always adjust the percentage to fit the remaining value. Smoothing vs. Catch-up: In the Company Code settings for Asset Accounting, check if Smoothing is active. PT04 is custom right.

entry.content:
Hi @barbara_hartel ,

I would suggest that you link to the Blog post from @andreas-roessler who provides the details how a Theme can be transported between Subaccounts using the SAP Content Agent Service: Effortlessly Sharing Themes Across Your SAP BTP Cloud Foundry Environments.

entry.title: Re: Effortlessly Sharing Themes Across Your SAP BTP Cloud Foundry Environments
entry.content:
Hi @andreas-roessler ,

I can't agree with the "well-explaine" in the documentation. I just had to setup SAP Content Agent Service and unfortunately it's a lot of try and error. I haven't found any mentioining of the Content Type that needs to be configured in the Transport Node. The correct one is "Multi-Target Application". Also I would suggest that you re-draw the picture in this post and increase the Subaccount box also arround Theming and CAS. The CAS Instance must be subscribed in the Subaccount where the Theme Designer is used. And the same needs to be applied for the Target Landscape where not only CAS needs to be subscribed but also an instance of the CAS with the Plan: application must be created. The service Key of this instance must be used to create the destination in the cTMS Subaccount.

Best regards,
Gregor

entry.content:

There is a 10x scaling discrepancy in the currency values for the field DMBTR_STOCK (Amount in LC w/ Stock Quantity) in SAP HANA Database.

  • SAP SE16N View: The value displays as 4.032,425.
  • Data Visualization/Raw MATDOC in SAP HANA DB: The value is appearing as 40,324.25 but actually it should be 4,032.425

entry.title: Product Compliance / About content and what you may be expect to get
entry.content:
Introduction

We can see many Blogs regarding "Content" in Product Compliance.

First: we need to define: is there a difference regarding "Content" in Product Compliance compared to SAP EHS classic?

The answer is: partielly there is a difference. 

First you need to understand: what content comes via SAP and what content not.

And we need to define the "content".

In old days using 3E as an example content can be defined as:

  • you are getting new phrases or new phrase translations
  • you are getting new rule sets or updates of rule sets
  • you get new WWI templates and updates of WWI templates
  • you get new legal contetent or updates on legal content (including e.g. Dangerous goods content)
  • you get an update of property trees

Using this definition of content: this is now different (based on best knowledge)

On long term you are getting updates of compliance requirement and/or legal content via SAP.  This can include: new phrases and new translations of phrases and include new "CAS" numbers and update of legal content for the "CAS" numbers.

What is the same.. SAP (best knowledge) will not deliver "rulesets"  or "WWI" like templates. Here you are free to select a content provider.

What will be as well different: User Experience is different... And this can help to manage the content.

SAP plans do delivery "AI" and agents so what data maintenance can be supported. This is a new functionality not available wirh EHS classic.  What exactly we will get.. we need to wait. But this is a kind of new functionality and to a certain extent you might call this a new content. 

But we need to wait at laetss for on premise version 2027 (or a clod version) to see; what is delivered, how flexible this might be and to whcih extent the new content will help.

Links

https://community.sap.com/t5/product-lifecycle-management-blog-posts-by-sap/agentic-ai-at-the-2026-sap-ehs-and-product-compliance-conference/ba-p/14368872

https://community.sap.com/t5/supply-chain-management-blog-posts-by-sap/product-compliance-in-sap-s-4hana-how-three-integrated-capabilities-enable/ba-p/14330707

https://community.sap.com/t5/product-lifecycle-management-blog-posts-by-sap/new-content-updates-for-product-compliance/ba-p/14307206

https://community.sap.com/t5/product-lifecycle-management-blog-posts-by-sap/inside-the-regulatory-content-service-everything-you-can-access/ba-p/14308587

https://community.sap.com/t5/supply-chain-management-blog-posts-by-sap/in-brief-product-compliance-dangerous-goods-management-s-4hana-end-to-end/ba-p/14376897

https://community.sap.com/t5/product-lifecycle-management-blog-posts-by-sap/dangerous-goods-compliance-from-regulatory-foundations-to-integration/ba-p/14289224

https://community.sap.com/t5/product-lifecycle-management-blog-posts-by-sap/navigating-the-new-milestone-in-continuous-delivery-of-regulatory-content/ba-p/14167666

entry.title: Re: im ERM wird die EKG in den Banf-Belegen nicht richtig...
entry.content:
Hallo @Markus_Boehm46 ,

mein Freund, dieses Problem tritt üblicherweise auf, wenn die Einkaufsgruppe in den zugrunde liegenden SAP-Tabellen (Bestellanforderungskopf/-position) fehlt oder inkonsistent ist, oder wenn die Extraktionslogik auf Ihrer Übersichtsseite das falsche Feld ausliest oder auf unvollständige Stammdaten stößt. Die Ursache liegt fast immer in unvollständigen Bestellanforderungspositionsdaten, einer fehlerhaften Konfiguration oder doppelten/falschen Einträgen in Mapping-Tabellen wie MMPUR_V_MG2PGRP.

Um die Fehlerursache zu finden, können Sie Folgendes überprüfen:

1. Prüfen Sie, ob die Bestellanforderungsposition tatsächlich eine Einkaufsgruppe enthält. Prüfen Sie die Tabelle EBAN, Feld EKGRP, für die betroffenen Bestellanforderungen. Wenn das Feld leer ist, kann die Extraktionslogik keine Daten anzeigen.

2. Prüfen Sie die Konfiguration der Einkaufsgruppenableitung. Insbesondere wenn Ihr System eine Materialgruppen-zu-Einkaufsgruppen-Zuordnung verwendet, prüfen Sie die Tabelle MMPUR_V_MG2PGRP. Entfernen Sie doppelte oder falsche Einträge. Stellen Sie sicher, dass die korrekte Materialgruppe der korrekten Einkaufsgruppe zugeordnet ist.

(SAP-Benutzer bestätigten dies als häufigste Ursache.)

3. Prüfen Sie, ob die Bestellanforderung aus einem Vertrag oder einer Bezugsquelle erstellt wurde. SAP überträgt die Einkaufsgruppe nicht automatisch von Verträgen in Bestellanforderungen.
Dies ist Standardverhalten.

4. Prüfen Sie das Layout Ihrer Dokumentübersicht. Stellen Sie sicher, dass die Spalte „Beleg mit Einkaufsgruppe“ tatsächlich den Wert EKGRP aus der Bestellanforderungsposition enthält und nicht etwa ein Kopfzeilenfeld oder ein benutzerdefiniertes CDS-Feld, das möglicherweise leer ist. Das SAP-Hilfeportal bestätigt, dass die angezeigten Felder von der ausgewählten Variante abhängen.

5. Prüfen Sie die Stammdaten: Ist im Materialstamm eine Einkaufsgruppe hinterlegt? Ist im Lieferantenstamm eine Einkaufsgruppe hinterlegt? Lässt sich im Belegtyp eine Einkaufsgruppe zulassen oder ist diese standardmäßig hinterlegt?

Hoffentlich lässt sich das Problem mit einer der oben genannten Ursachen beheben. Wenn Ihnen meine Lösung zusagt, akzeptieren Sie sie bitte.

entry.title: Re: I am getting a warning message while opening a work o...
entry.content:
Hi @sankets1 ,
My friend, you are seeing the warning because SAP is detecting the deletion flag on that specific material in the context of this order, while the other orders/components do not trigger the same check due to different master‑data or order‑data conditions. This behavior is consistent with SAP’s standard logic for message IM049 – “Assembly & is flagged for deletion”

The reason of warning could be the following as mentioned below.
1. The material is flagged for deletion (client‑level, plant‑level, or storage‑location level).
2. The material is used as an assembly/component in the order.
3. The system performs a deletion‑flag check for that specific order type/status.

From the SAP documentation on IM049, the message is triggered when an assembly or component is flagged for deletion in the material master and is still being used in a transaction such as a work order

The material is maintained as a component in the same order. The deletion flag is set at client level. Other orders with similar components do not show the warning.

My suggestion, SAP is treating this specific component as an “assembly” in this order, while in other orders it is treated as a normal component.
This can happen if, when the component has a different BOM usage or special procurement key. The order type triggers assembly processing logic. The component has Make‑to‑Order (MTO) or collective order settings. There is an inconsistency in the order header or component assignment.

entry.title: Troubleshooting Email Output in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition (1) - Email not Reaching Recipient
entry.content:
1. Overview

A very common and confusing situation in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition is when everything on the SAP side looks perfectly fine: the output item shows status Completed in the business document, and the Monitor Email Transmissions app shows the email as Sent, yet the recipient never receives the email.

This does not indicate a bug in SAP. It means the email was handed off by the SAP system to the mail infrastructure, but was then silently rejected or filtered as spam by the recipient's mail server before it reached the inbox. Because SAP has no visibility of what happens after handoff, it still reports Sent or Completed.

The root cause in virtually all such cases comes down to the below two missing email security configurations that must be set up on the customer side:

  • SPF (Sender Policy Framework)
  • DKIM (DomainKeys Identified Mail)

2. SPF (Sender Policy Framework) - What It Is and How to Set It Up

2.1 What is SPF?

SPF is an email validation system designed to prevent email spam by detecting email spoofing, a common vulnerability, through verifying sender IP addresses. SPF allows administrators to specify the IP address of the sending email servers that are authorized to send email with Envelope From addresses in the sender domain. SPF does this by creating a specific SPF record (or TXT record) in the Domain Name System (DNS).

To put it in a more direct way, SPF is a DNS TXT record published on your domain that declares which IP addresses and mail servers are authorized to send email on your behalf. When a recipient mail server receives an email claiming to be from your domain, it checks your SPF record. If the sending server is not listed, the email may be rejected or flagged as spam.

2.2 How to Check Whether Your SPF Record Is Already Published

You can use a free online diagnostic tool such as MX Toolbox to check:

  1. Go to: https://mxtoolbox.com/SuperTool.aspx.
  2. Provide the domain in the format of myXXXXXX.mail.s4hana.ondemand.com or myXXXXXX.mail.saps4hanacloud.cn.

The above two steps check whether the DNS TXT record settings are already in place. If not, go on with step 2.3 below.

2.3 How to Publish the SPF Record

Work with your DNS administrator or network team to create a SPF record (or TXT record) in the sender DNS, by specifying IPs from the previous step. A typical SPF record for SAP S/4HANA Cloud looks like:

v=spf1 include:_spf.cmail.ondemand.com ~all

This instructs recipient mail servers that SAP's mail infrastructure (cmail.ondemand.com) is authorised to send mail on behalf of your domain.

For the complete instructions, check the chapter 4.2.6 Check the SPF Record for Sender Domain of Setting Up Output Management (1LQ).

3. DKIM (DomainKeys Identified Mail) - What It Is and How to Set It Up

3.1 What is DKIM and Why Is It Mandatory?

DKIM is an email authentication technique that attaches a digital signature to every outgoing email. The signature is generated using a private key held by the sending mail server (SAP's infrastructure). The recipient mail server verifies the signature using a public key published in your DNS, confirming two things:

  • The email was genuinely sent by an authorised server for your domain.
  • The email content was not altered in transit between sending and receiving servers.

DKIM setup became mandatory in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition as of release 2208. Without it, emails sent from the system - especially from a custom sender domain - will likely not be delivered reliably.

3.2 How to Request DKIM Activation (Step by Step)

DKIM activation is a two-stage process that involves both the customer and SAP Cloud Operations. It cannot be self-served and requires raising a service request.

Stage 1: SAP generates the DKIM key and the customer updates their DNS

  1. Log on to SAP for Me.
  2. Under Dashboards, choose Services & Support.
  3. On the Service Requests tab, choose New Service Request. The system displays the New Service Request dialog.
  4. Choose the Partner & Other Cloud Services radio button, then choose Create Service Request.
  5. Search for SAP S/4HANA Cloud: DKIM enablement for custom sender domains and open the service request.
  6. Read the service-related information and choose Add Service.
  7. Under Tenants, specify the relevant tenant for your SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition system (recommended: customizing tenant).
  8. In the Enter the Domain Name field, enter a domain name according to your business requirements.
  9. A confirmation text for your service request is displayed. Under Confirmation, select the checkbox to confirm that you meet the prerequisites for the service, or you agree to the guidelines as per the SAP Standards, and that you are ready to proceed with the service.
  10. Choose Submit to send your service request to SAP

Alternatively, you can raise a support ticket directly to component XX-S4C-OPR-SRV using the following template:

"Hello SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Operations,
Please set up and activate DKIM to emails sent from my SAP S/4HANA system.
SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition system: Please provide the SIDs or host names or URLs of your SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition system from which you plan to use DKIM. For example: <myXXXXXX.s4hana.com>
I want to enable DKIM for the domain <your domain> and top-level-domain <your top-level-domain>. For example: company.com
Thanks,
<Your Name>"

SAP Cloud Operations will generate the DKIM key. This takes approximately 3 to 4 hours. Once ready, they will provide you with the key to update your DNS.

Note: For reference screenshots of DNS update steps, see KBA 3231960. Note that DNS providers vary - the screenshots are for reference only.

Stage 2: DNS validation and DKIM profile activation

  1. Update your DNS with the DKIM key provided by SAP. Work with your network or mail server team - DNS is provider-specific and SAP does not manage your DNS.
  2. Once DNS is updated, reply to the SAP support case to inform Cloud Operations.
  3. SAP Cloud Operations will validate DNS entry. If correct, they will activate the DKIM profile. This also takes approximately 3 to 4 hours.
  4. After activation, verify DKIM by checking the email header of a test message - look for a DKIM-Signature field.

4. Frequently Asked Questions

See KBA 3397415 - FAQs about DKIM activation for a domain for S/4HANA Public Cloud Customer

5. Related KBAs and Resources

  • 3274509 - Emails do not reach the recipients - SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
  • 3265907 - Output Items of Email channel are completed but emails do not reach recipient
  • 3223182 - Mandatory DKIM setup - SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
  • 3231960 - How to Update the DNS with the provided DKIM Key
  • 3397415 - FAQs about DKIM activation for a domain for S/4HANA Public Cloud Customer
  • 2941522 - Output Management in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition - Guided Answer
  • Setting Up Output Management (1LQ) - SAP Best Practices Explorer
  • Monitor Email Transmission - SAP Fiori App Reference

entry.title: Re: How to allocate Capacity Units (CU) to SAC user licenses
entry.content:
You don’t allocate Capacity Units (CU) directly to SAC users — that’s not how the model works.

 Key point

CU is a shared resource pool at tenant level, not something you assign per user.

In the SAP Analytics Cloud capacity-based model:

  • You purchase CU as a bundle (compute, memory, storage)
  • You assign roles to users → roles determine license type
  • CU consumption is then calculated automatically based on license mix and usage, not manually assigned

 What you actually control

  • Number of users per license type (BI, Planning Standard, Planning Professional)
  • Role assignments (which define license consumption)
  • Overall CU sizing using the estimator

For example:

  • Adding more Planning Professional users increases CU consumption
  • BI users consume less CU compared to planning users

 What you cannot do

  • Assign “X CU to User A”
  • Set CU quotas per individual user
  • Track CU usage per specific user

Why SAP designed it this way

CU reflects actual workload usage, not fixed per-user limits. This allows:

  • Flexible scaling
  • Mixed usage patterns (heavy vs light users)
  • Better optimization across the whole tenant

 Simple takeaway

  • CU = shared pool (tenant-level)
  • Users = assigned licenses via roles
  • Allocation = indirect (through license types), not per user

entry.title: Re: Focus of this Learning Group and Best Practices for Using the Forum
entry.content: Thanks for setting these clear guidelines, Philip! Establishing a structured way to reference course IDs and modules will definitely streamline how we support each other in this group. Having gone through the certification journeys for both EWM and TM myself, I know how crucial it is to have focused, actionable discussions when tackling the SAP Learning Hub exercises. Looking forward to exchanging ideas and helping fellow learners out where I can!

entry.title: Re: Problem reinstalling SAP ASE
entry.content:
Reinstall issues in SAP ASE usually happen because the previous installation was not fully cleaned up.

Most common causes are:

  • Leftover ASE folders or system files still present
  • Port or server name already in use
  • Old environment variables (like SYBASE) not removed
  • Installer trying to reuse existing configuration

Simple fix:

  1. Stop ASE services
  2. Remove old ASE directories completely
  3. Check and free used ports
  4. Clean environment variables
  5. Reboot system
  6. Run installer again as admin/root

 In most cases, a clean removal + fresh install resolves the issue.

entry.title: Re: Usage of Catalog Items outside of Purchase Requisitions

entry.title: Re: SAP BUSINESS APPLICATION STUDIO not working as expected.
entry.content:
SAP Business Application Studio issues are usually related to browser, connectivity, or service environment problems, not just the tool itself.

From similar cases, common causes are:

  • BAS service not fully initialized or temporarily down
  • Browser cache or session issues
  • Network / VPN blocking access to SAP BTP services
  • Missing entitlements or space configuration issues in BTP
  • Extension or workspace runtime problems

🔧 Quick things to try:

  • Refresh or reopen BAS in a new browser session
  • Clear browser cache / try incognito mode
  • Check BTP cockpit → ensure BAS service is running
  • Restart dev space (stop → start again)
  • Try a different browser (Chrome/Edge)

SAP also notes that BAS depends heavily on stable connectivity to its backend services, so network/service interruptions can cause blank screens or unexpected behavior.

🧠 Simple takeaway

Most BAS “not working as expected” issues are caused by session, connectivity, or dev space runtime problems, and restarting or recreating the dev space often resolves it.

entry.title: SAP Joule for Developers: Your AI Copilot for Smarter, Faster SAP Development
entry.content:
Introduction

Every SAP developer knows the feeling — staring at thousands of lines of legacy ABAP code, manually writing unit tests, or spending hours trying to understand a CDS view someone else wrote three years ago. What if you had an AI copilot sitting right beside you in your development environment, understanding your context, generating code, explaining logic, and helping you migrate faster?

That's exactly what SAP Joule for Developers delivers.

Introduced in early 2025 and rapidly expanding its reach, Joule for Developers is SAP's embedded AI capability suite designed specifically for the needs of SAP developers. Whether you work with ABAP, Java, JavaScript, low-code tools, or process automation — Joule is now your intelligent development partner. And the best part? It's currently free of charge.

What Is SAP Joule for Developers?

SAP Joule for Developers is the collective name for design-time AI capabilities across SAP Build and ABAP that help improve developer productivity and proficiency. It covers AI assistance built directly into:

  • SAP Build Code — Full-stack app development with Java, JavaScript, and CAP
  • SAP Build Process Automation — AI-assisted workflow and automation creation
  • SAP Build Apps — Low-code/no-code app development with AI support
  • ABAP Environment — AI capabilities embedded in ABAP Development Tools for Eclipse (ADT)

Important distinction: Joule for Developers focuses exclusively on design-time AI — meaning AI that helps you during development. It does not include runtime AI capabilities like SAP Document AI, SAP AI Core, or SAP Build Work Zone's AI features.

 

Why Joule for Developers Is a Game Changer

Purpose-Built for SAP

Unlike generic AI coding assistants, Joule for Developers is powered by purpose-built large language models specifically trained on SAP code and processes, including a dedicated ABAP LLM trained on millions of lines of enterprise-grade ABAP code. This means:

  • It understands SAP-specific syntax, frameworks, and best practices
  • It respects ABAP language version contexts (e.g., ABAP Cloud vs. classic ABAP)
  • It delivers accurate, contextual results tailored to SAP development scenarios — not generic programming suggestions

Proven Business Impact

SAP reports that Joule for Developers can reduce development costs by up to 30%. Partners and customers who have adopted it are already seeing measurable results:

"Joule for Developers dramatically boosts productivity by enabling developers to quickly generate enterprise-grade apps, including logic, data models, sample data, and test scripts." — Gerard Van Paassen, CEO, Aarini Consulting

"PwC uses Joule for Developers in SAP Build to rapidly deliver innovative SAP solutions. Its generative AI supercharges our developers' output, helping us accelerate award-winning ESG solutions." — Dr. [Name], PwC

 

Key Capabilities: What Can Joule for Developers Do?

  1. Code Generation & Full-Stack App Creation

Generate complete full-stack applications from natural language prompts — including data models, business logic, UI components, and sample data — all following SAP programming models like RAP (RESTful ABAP Programming Model) and CAP (Cloud Application Programming Model).

  1. Predictive Code Completion

Get real-time, context-aware code suggestions as you type in your development environment. Joule predicts and generates subsequent lines of code based on your ABAPDoc comments, existing code patterns, and the ABAP language version of your current development object.

  1. Code Explanation & Understanding

Instantly understand what a piece of code does — even if you didn't write it. Joule can explain:

  • CDS view entities, classes, interfaces, and function modules
  • Legacy ABAP programs and reports
  • Business purpose and implementation logic of unknown code objects

This is invaluable for onboarding new developers and tackling inherited codebases.

  1. Unit Test & CDS Test Generation

Automatically generate ABAP unit tests and CDS unit tests based on your business logic. Catch issues earlier in the lifecycle and ensure more reliable application behavior in production — without manually writing every test case.

  1. Code Refactoring & Optimization

Use natural language prompts to refactor existing code, improve quality, and align with SAP best practices — all while keeping your ABAP cloud-compliant and clean-core-ready.

  1. Process & Automation Generation

In SAP Build Process Automation, Joule can generate complete automation workflows, business rules, forms, and scripts from natural language queries — dramatically reducing time spent on repetitive configuration tasks.

  1. OData Consumption Assistance

Get AI-assisted guidance on consuming OData services in your ABAP Cloud developments — one of the more complex tasks for developers new to ABAP Cloud patterns.

 

Big News: Joule for Developers Expands to Private Cloud

One of the most significant milestones in 2025 was the expansion of Joule for Developers ABAP AI capabilities to SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition 2025. Previously available only for BTP ABAP Environment and SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud Edition, this expansion means private cloud customers can now also leverage the full power of AI-assisted ABAP development.

What's New for Private Cloud?

This release brings a full set of ABAP Cloud development capabilities to Private Cloud 2025, including:

  • Joule Copilot integrated into ABAP Development Tools for Eclipse
  • Predictive Code Completion
  • ABAP / CDS Code Explanation
  • ABAP Unit Test Generation
  • CDS Unit Test Generation
  • ABAP Cloud Application Generation
  • OData Consumption support

Custom Code Migration: A Major Leap Forward

The Private Cloud release also introduces the first wave of Custom Code Migration capabilities — a set of AI-powered features designed to accelerate the move from classic SAP ERP to SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition:

Documentation Chat: Get instant, accurate answers about SAP S/4HANA simplifications and the impact they have on your existing custom code. No more digging through simplification lists manually.

ABAP Program Explain: Automatically understand the business purpose and implementation logic of legacy ABAP programs and reports — essential when dealing with decades-old custom code that lacks documentation.

Explain for SAP S/4HANA Test Cockpit Issues: Diagnose and resolve ATC (ABAP Test Cockpit) findings related to S/4HANA simplification items, with step-by-step guidance on how to fix each issue using context from simplification cookbooks.

This is a massive time-saver for organizations currently on their S/4HANA transformation journey.

The ABAP AI SDK: Build Your Own AI-Powered ABAP Applications

Beyond the developer tooling, Joule for Developers also includes the ABAP AI SDK — a standardized SDK that allows ABAP developers to integrate AI capabilities directly into their own custom ABAP applications and extensions.

The ABAP AI SDK is:

  • Used internally by SAP for its own ABAP AI functionality
  • Easy to configure and consume — no deep AI expertise required
  • Available across environments — BTP ABAP Environment, S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition 2025, and even on-premise via Transport-based Correction Instructions (TCIs)

This opens the door to building intelligent, AI-powered custom applications on top of SAP AI Core — directly from within ABAP.

Where Is Joule for Developers Available?

Environment

Availability

SAP BTP ABAP Environment

✅ Generally Available

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

✅ Generally Available

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition 2025

✅ Generally Available (as of Oct 2025)

SAP S/4HANA On-Premise (lower releases)

✅ Via Transport-based Correction Instructions (TCI) — SAP Note 3513374

SAP Build Code

✅ Enabled by default

SAP Build Process Automation

✅ Enable from Build Lobby

SAP Build Apps

✅ Enable from Build Lobby

How to Get Access: It's Free!

SAP Joule for Developers is available free of charge through Q3 2026 — a promotional offer that SAP has extended to ensure every customer and partner can explore and adopt AI-assisted development without commercial barriers.

For SAP Build (Build Code, Build Process Automation, Build Apps):

  • SAP Build Code: AI capabilities are enabled by default when you sign up
  • SAP Build Process Automation & SAP Build Apps: Enable from the Build Lobby → Control Tower → Generative AI Settings → Toggle "Enable AI" → Accept SAP AI Terms

For ABAP Environment (BTP / Public Cloud / Private Cloud 2025):

  1. Request the no-cost service SKU via your SAP Account Executive or through the Manage My Services App:
    • Customers: Material Number 8019124
    • TDD Partners: Material Number 8019541
  2. Once the order is processed, ABAP development systems are enabled within 48 hours
  3. Assign required authorizations to your development users

Note: A valid SAP Build or ABAP license is required as a prerequisite. After the promotional period ends, a commercial model based on AI Units will apply.

Frequently Asked Questions

Q: Is Joule for Developers the same as SAP Joule (the end-user copilot)?

No. SAP Joule is the end-user AI copilot embedded across SAP applications for business users. Joule for Developers is specifically for developers — focused on design-time activities like code generation, testing, and code understanding within development tools.

Q: Does it work with non-SAP systems too?

Yes. Joule capabilities in SAP Build can help build and extend applications across both SAP and non-SAP systems, as confirmed by partners like PwC and Deloitte.

Q: What about data privacy and security?

Joule for Developers uses SAP's purpose-built, enterprise-grade AI models within the SAP AI Core infrastructure. Accepting the SAP AI Terms is a prerequisite for activation, ensuring appropriate governance.

Q: Can I use it on lower releases of S/4HANA Private Cloud or On-Premise?

Yes, for lower releases of S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition and On-Premise systems, ABAP AI capabilities can be deployed via Transport-based Correction Instructions (TCIs). Refer to SAP Note 3513374 for details.

Getting Started: Your Action Plan

Ready to bring AI into your ABAP development workflow? Here's how to start today:

  1. Check your environment: Confirm you're on a supported platform (BTP ABAP, S/4HANA Public or Private Cloud 2025, or On-Premise with TCIs)
  2. Request the license: Contact your SAP Account Executive for the no-cost SKU (8019124 for customers)
  3. Enable AI in SAP Build: Follow the quick setup in the Build Lobby if you're using Build Process Automation or Build Apps
  4. Open ADT (Eclipse): Once enabled, the Joule copilot will be available directly in your ABAP Development Tools
  5. Explore: Start with code explanation on a legacy program or generate a simple unit test — the results will speak for themselves

Conclusion

SAP Joule for Developers represents a fundamental shift in how SAP developers work. From generating entire ABAP Cloud applications to explaining decades-old legacy code and accelerating S/4HANA migrations, the capabilities are both broad and deeply SAP-specific.

With the expansion to Private Cloud 2025, the introduction of Custom Code Migration features, and the availability of the ABAP AI SDK — SAP is making clear that AI assistance isn't a future concept for SAP developers; it's available right now, and it's free.

If you're an ABAP developer, a Build developer, or an SAP architect helping your organization stay competitive in the AI era — there has never been a better time to get started with Joule for Developers.

Have you tried Joule for Developers yet? Share your experience in the comments — I'd love to hear which capabilities are making the biggest difference for your team!

References:

  • SAP Community Blog: SAP Joule for Developers Expands to Private Cloud
  • SAP Community FAQ: SAP Joule for Developers — Features, Access and More
  • SAP News: Introducing Joule for Developers: AI-Powered Capabilities Across SAP
  • SAP Product Page: SAP Joule for Developers
  • SAP Note: 3513374 (TCI-based deployment for lower S/4HANA releases)

entry.title: Unlock the Power of Intelligent Document Processing with SAP Document AI and Generative AI
entry.content:
Introduction

In today's fast-paced enterprise world, documents are everywhere — invoices, purchase orders, delivery notes, contracts, and more. Processing them manually is time-consuming, error-prone, and simply not scalable. That's where SAP Document AI comes in.

SAP Document AI (formerly known as Document Information Extraction) is a service on the SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP) that uses machine learning and, more recently, Large Language Models (LLMs) to automatically extract structured information from business documents. With the integration of Generative AI, it has become more powerful than ever — capable of handling not just standard document types, but virtually any custom document you throw at it.

In this blog post, I'll walk you through how SAP Document AI works, how to leverage Generative AI for document extraction, and why this matters for your SAP landscape.

What Is SAP Document AI?

SAP Document AI is an intelligent document processing service that enables businesses to:

  • Automatically extract key fields from documents (header fields, line items, etc.)
  • Support standard document types like invoices, payment advices, and purchase orders out of the box
  • Handle custom document types (delivery notes, birth certificates, contracts, etc.) using schema-based extraction
  • Integrate seamlessly with SAP S/4HANA, SAP Ariba, and other SAP solutions via API

At its core, the service uses pretrained machine learning models that are globally trained on vast datasets of business documents. But the real game-changer? The addition of Generative AI.

Generative AI + SAP Document AI: A Powerful Combination

Why Generative AI?

Traditional ML models are great for standard document types where the structure is well-known. But what about non-standard documents that vary in layout, language, or structure? This is where LLMs shine.

By combining SAP Document AI with Generative AI (LLMs), you can:

  • Define custom schemas for any document type without training a dedicated ML model
  • Use natural language descriptions as prompts to guide the LLM on what to extract
  • Get accurate extraction results even for documents with varying formats
  • Reduce the time-to-value dramatically — no lengthy ML training cycles required

Two Extraction Approaches in One Service

SAP Document AI now supports a hybrid approach:

Setup Type

Method

Best For

auto with Default Extractor

Pretrained ML Model

Standard fields in invoices, POs, payment advices

auto without Default Extractor

Generative AI (LLM)

Custom fields, non-standard documents

manual

Template-based

Highly structured, fixed-layout documents

This flexibility means you can mix and match extraction methods within the same schema, getting the best of both worlds.

How It Works: Step-by-Step

Here's a simplified walkthrough of how to use SAP Document AI with Generative AI:

Step 1: Set Up Your Environment

Start with an SAP BTP Trial or Free Tier account. Use the SAP BTP Booster to automatically provision the SAP Document AI service instance and subscribe to the Basic UI application. This sets up everything you need to get started quickly.

Step 2: Create a Schema

A schema defines the structure of the data you want to extract. Think of it as a template that tells the AI what information to look for.

  • Navigate to Schema Configuration in the SAP Document AI Basic UI

 

  • Click Create and give your schema a name (e.g., delivery_note_schema)

 

  • Select the appropriate Document Type — choose Custom for non-standard documents, or a specific type like Invoice for standard documents

Step 3: Define Your Fields

This is where Generative AI truly shines. For each field you want to extract:

 

  • Enter a field name (e.g., vendor_name, total_amount, delivery_date)
  • Choose a data type (string, number, date, currency, etc.)
  • Add a description — this is used as a prompt for the LLM

💡 Pro Tip: The description field is your prompt to the LLM. Write it as if you're explaining what you want to extract to a person with no prior context. The more specific you are, the better the extraction results!

For example:

  • Field: invoice_number
  • Description: "The unique identifier assigned to this invoice by the issuing company, typically found in the header area of the document."

Step 4: Activate the Schema

Once all fields are added, Activate the schema to move it from DRAFT to ACTIVE status. Only active schemas can be used for document extraction.

 

Step 5: Upload Documents and Extract

  • Navigate to the Document section in the UI

 

  • Upload your document (PDF, image, etc.)
  • Select your schema
  • The service processes the document and returns extracted field values in seconds

 

Real-World Use Cases

The combination of SAP Document AI and Generative AI opens up exciting possibilities:

  1. Accounts Payable Automation Automatically extract invoice details (vendor, amount, tax, line items) and post directly to SAP S/4HANA — reducing manual data entry and processing time.
  2. Logistics & Supply Chain Process delivery notes and shipping documents of any format, extracting quantities, dates, and goods information without custom ML training.
  3. HR & Compliance Extract information from onboarding documents, certificates, or contracts — even in multiple languages and varying formats.
  4. Cross-Industry Document Processing From healthcare (medical reports) to legal (contracts) to finance (bank statements) — if it's a document, SAP Document AI can handle it.

Integration with SAP Ecosystem

SAP Document AI isn't a standalone tool — it's designed to integrate deeply into the SAP ecosystem:

  • REST API: Integrate with any application using the standard SAP Document AI REST API via Swagger UI
  • SAP BTP CAP Applications: Build custom CAP-based applications that consume the Document AI service for automated document workflows
  • SAP Build: Use SAP Build Code or SAP Build Apps to create no-code/low-code automations powered by Document AI
  • SAP S/4HANA & Ariba: Direct integration for invoice processing and procurement document handling

Getting Started: Try It for Free

The best part? You can try SAP Document AI today at no cost using the SAP BTP Trial or Free Tier:

  1. Sign up for SAP BTP Trial
  2. Use the SAP BTP Booster to set up SAP Document AI
  3. Follow the hands-on tutorial on SAP Developers: Extract Information from Custom Documents with Generative AI

The trial provides a sandbox environment where you can experiment with both standard and custom document types using real sample files.

Key Takeaways

  • SAP Document AI combines machine learning and Generative AI (LLMs) for flexible, accurate document extraction
  • Custom schemas allow you to extract information from virtually any document type — no ML training required
  • Field descriptions act as LLM prompts — writing good descriptions directly improves extraction accuracy
  • The service is available on SAP BTP Trial and Free Tier, making it easy to experiment and build proof-of-concepts
  • Deep integration with the SAP ecosystem enables seamless end-to-end document automation

Conclusion

SAP Document AI with Generative AI is a transformative capability for any organization dealing with high volumes of documents. By removing the need for custom ML training while still delivering accurate structured extraction, it dramatically lowers the barrier to intelligent document processing.

Whether you're looking to automate accounts payable, streamline logistics, or build a custom document processing workflow — SAP Document AI gives you the tools to do it efficiently on SAP BTP.

I encourage you to try the hands-on tutorial and explore what's possible. Have questions or want to share your experience? Drop a comment below — I'd love to hear how you're using SAP Document AI in your projects!

References:

  • SAP Developers Tutorial: Extract Information from Custom Documents with Generative AI and SAP Document AI
  • SAP Developers Tutorial: Extract Information from Standard Documents with Generative AI and SAP Document AI
  • SAP Document AI on SAP BTP: Free Tier Setup

entry.title: Enable the excel template and import excel to upload the stocklevel data in Backoffice -SAP Commerce
entry.content:
I want to enable the download excel template and import excel to upload the stocklevel data in backoffice - SAP commerce cloud.

I enabled the download excel template and import excel by making changes in the backoffice-config.xml. But the problem is the stocklevel has a relation with warehouse and the warehouse in turn has relation with vendor. In the downloaded excel file I donot see the warehouse attribute in the column dropdown and without warehouse attribute we cannot import the stock level excel.

Debugged the code and found that there are filters that check for unique attribute and if its unique then those attributes gets enabled in excel.

Tried to make unique modifier true for code attribute in warehouse itemtype in the items.xml, but it didnot work, tried using redeclare as well, no luck. Below is the error

core-customize/hybris/bin/platform/resources/ant/antmacros.xml:587: java.lang.IllegalArgumentException: cannot merge namespace ((examplecore)) into ((<merged>)) due to duplicate attribute 'Warehouse.code(java.lang.String):((examplecore))::YAttributeDescriptor[examplecore-items.xml:519(AttributeTagListener)][PROPERTY]' vs 'Warehouse.code(java.lang.String):((basecommerce))::YAttributeDescriptor[basecommerce-items.xml:941(AttributeTagListener)][PROPERTY]'

So did it via, the impex:


UPDATE RelationDescriptor; enclosingType(code)[unique = true]; qualifier[unique = true]; unique
                          ; StockLevel                          ; warehouse                   ; true

UPDATE AttributeDescriptor; enclosingType(code)[unique = true]; qualifier[unique = true]; unique
                          ; Warehouse                          ; code                   ; true

UPDATE RelationDescriptor; enclosingType(code)[unique = true]; qualifier[unique = true]; unique
                         ; Warehouse                          ; vendor                   ; true

UPDATE AttributeDescriptor; enclosingType(code)[unique = true]; qualifier[unique = true]; unique
                          ; Vendor                          ; code                   ; true

After making the above changes, I see the attributes in excel template as required below:

But now the problem is when the build is deployed with no migrate data and in rolling update mode, the impex will not be run and modifiers of attributes flushes back to the initial configuration of unique as false for all the above attribute and relation descriptor.

Can some one help me in how to solve this issue??

entry.title: Re: All thw DTPs are in inactive state post SPS upgrade
entry.content:
There is no standard BW process, that would inactivate DTP-s specifically during an upgrade process. The DTP-s were either already inactive prior to the upgrade or someone did something which has lead to the deactivation (impact handling).

Even a manual systemcopy can easily lead to inactive DTP-s if not all steps are performed properly. This should not happen when PCA is used.

Btw RSDG_TRFN_ACTIVATE can be used to activate TRFN-s + DTP-s.

entry.title: Re: Create New Catalog, Space, Page, Section and Role – S4HANA Private Cloud
entry.content:
I have some observations.

1) Why posting this blog twice under the same tag?

Create New Catalog, Space, Page, Section and Role ... - SAP Community

Create New Catalog, Space, Page, Section and Role ... - SAP Community

2) There is nothing special on handling Fiori authorizations in S/4HANA Cloud Private edition (which btw is not written "S4HANA Private Cloud" as the title suggests)  other than on-premise edition.

3) This step by step uses /UI2/FLPD_CUST which is obsolete for at least 3 years already. Some snapshots on this tutorial partially show the messages where it says that.

4) There is nothing in this blog which is not yet very well documented and posted before. 

5) References should be done using /UI2/FLPCM_CONF or /UI2/FLPCM_CUST and not from the designer.

6) The post links the old Fiori Apps Library

Old: https://fioriappslibrary.hana.ondemand.com/sap/fix/externalViewer/

New: https://pr.alm.me.sap.com/launchpad#FALApp-display

7) It does not make sense to create a Space, a page and a section for the very same thing: "GL Reports"

8 ) If someone follow this step by step the app would not work as there are authorization objects needed to display data in this app.

9) Creating a catalog for a single app is not wrong but if someone does this every time a new app is requested the authorization concept becomes a nightmare. There are cleaver ways of assinging apps to users which the blog does not even mention.

10) The post completely ignored other apps which might navigate to this app or other apps which are linked to it

11) The role was never assigned to the user so this step by step would not work 😉

12) The introduction uses the poor excuse that functional consultants don't have to depend on ABAP or BASIS to achieve overall objective. What objective? Accessing a Fiori app or running over IT process? How is that different than requesting acccess to a tcode which should and should not belong to FICO?

First, authorizations are not handled by ABAP or BASIS in decent organizations. Fiori and SAP authoriations are separate skills.

But as you want incentivate running over IT process, why not explaining that the app will only work once the necessary components have been installed by BASIS + the OData services have been activated by someone? The same thing goes for the launchpad and the Manage Launchpad Spaces and Pages apps. They will only work if someone else has set them up.

As you are a single person persuing your objective while others are blocking you, why not creating the profile on your own? Why not assiging roles to your fellows functional consultants? Why not exposing this app to WorkZone in BTP if it is used as a central launchpad?

13) In decent IT landscapes and process, if you find an app which you should have access to, you request it by a ticket or something for people who can do the job for you. What you don't do is to try to grant the access yourself.

Even if some ABAP developer (with decent Fiori skills) is maintaining catalogs and spaces, this should not give them rights to decide how authorizations should be organized. Nor a functional consultant without decent Fiori skills

Old: https://fioriappslibrary.hana.ondemand.com/sap/fix/externalViewer/

New: https://pr.alm.me.sap.com/launchpad#FALApp-display

7) It does not make sense to create a Space, a page and a section for the very same thing: "GL Reports"

8 ) If someone follow this step by step the app would not work as there are authorization objects needed to display data in this app.

9) Creating a catalog for a single app is not wrong but if someone does this every time a new app is requested the authorization concept becomes a nightmare. There are cleaver ways of assinging apps to users which the blog does not even mention.

10) The post completely ignored other apps which might navigate to this app or other apps which are linked to it

11) The role was never assigned to the user so this step by step would not work 

 

12) The introduction uses the poor excuse that functional consultants don't have to depend on ABAP or BASIS to achieve overall objective. What objective? Accessing a Fiori app or running over IT process? How is that different than requesting acccess to a tcode which should and should not belong to FICO?

First, authorizations are not handled by ABAP or BASIS in decent organizations. Fiori and SAP authoriations are separate skills.

But as you want incentivate running over IT process, why not explaining that the app will only work once the necessary components have been installed by BASIS + the OData services have been activated by someone? The same thing goes for the launchpad and the Manage Launchpad Spaces and Pages apps. They will only work if someone else has set them up.

As you are a single person persuing your objective while others are blocking you, why not creating the profile on your own? Why not assiging roles to your fellows functional consultants? Why not exposing this app to WorkZone in BTP if it is used as a central launchpad?

13) In decent IT landscapes and process, if you find an app which you should have access to, you request it by a ticket or something for people who can do the job for you. What you don't do is to try to grant the access yourself.

Even if some ABAP developer (with decent Fiori skills) is maintaining catalogs and spaces, this should not give them rights to decide how authorizations should be organized. Nor a functional consultant without decent Fiori skills

entry.title: Re: ACDOCA_M_EXTRACT vs MBEW
entry.content: For a stock report with per-unit price, you should not take the price directly from ACDOCA_M_EXTRACT. Instead, derive it from MBEW + relevant valuation tables, and then multiply with quantities from the stock tables.

entry.title: Troubleshooting Email Output in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition (2) – Four Frequent Errors
entry.content:
Overview

When setting up email output in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, several different errors can prevent emails from being sent. Unlike the scenario where emails appear as “Sent” but never arrive in the receiver’s inbox (an SPF/DKIM delivery issue, see the other blog Troubleshooting Email Output in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition (1) - Email not Reaching Recipient), the errors covered in this guide occur earlier in the process – the system refuses to even attempt sending the email because of missing or misconfigured settings.

This guide covers four common errors:

  • "No email recipient has been maintained"
  • "Recipient address x.y@abc.com not allowed"
  • "Sender domain in xxxx@xxxx.com is not allowed" / "Domain in sender email address is not allowed"
  • "Sender email address can't be initial"

Error 1: "No Email Recipient Has Been Maintained"

This error means the system could not find a recipient email address to send the output to. It affects the corresponding document types that use the EMAIL channel. The “To” field of the output item is empty.

The system looks for the recipient email address in two places, in order of priority:

  • First: A specific email address defined directly in the determination step “Email Recipient” of the Output Parameter Determination (OPD) app.
  • Second: If no DEFAULT email is maintained in OPD, the system reads the email address from the relevant business partner master data.

1.1 Fix in Output Parameter Determination (OPD)

  1. Open the Output Parameter Determination app and check the determination step “Email Recipient” for the relevant document type.
  2. Enter a specific recipient email address directly in the rule.

This takes the highest priority and overrides any address in the business partner master data.

1.2 Fix in Business Partner Master Data

If no default email is maintained in OPD, the email address must be maintained in the appropriate business partner master record. Where the email is stored depends on who the recipient is:

1.2.1 Recipient is a Customer (Taking Billing Document as an example):

  1. Open the Maintain Business Partner app.
  2. Search for the relevant customer business partner.
  3. Go to the “Address” tab.
  4. Ensure an email address is entered in the E-Mail field.

1.2.2 Recipient is a Contact Person (Taking Billing Document as an example):

  1. Open the Maintain Business Partner app and search with the customer number.
  2. Click the Relationships button.
  3. Find the relevant contact person and verify the Valid To date is set to 12/31/9999.
  4. Click the Change button.
  5. Under Address Data, click Assign Company Address.
  6. Enter the email address in the E-Mail field.

Note: If the contact person's Valid To date has expired, the system will not read the email address from that contact person record, even if an email is maintained there.

1.2.3 Recipient is a Supplier (Taking Payment Advice Document as an example):

For payment advice output processed through the Manage Automatic Payments app, the recipient email address is stored differently from customer outputs:

  1. Open the Maintain Business Partner app.
  2. Select the relevant business partner and the role “Supplier (Financial Accounting)”.
  3. Choose Company Code on the ribbon.
  4. Open the Vendor: Correspondence tab.
  5. Enter the recipient email address in the field Clrk's Internet add (Internet address of partner company clerk).

Error 2: "Recipient Address x.y@abc.com Not Allowed"

This error occurs in test systems only. SAP S/4HANA Cloud test systems have a built-in safeguard that restricts which recipient email domains the system is allowed to send emails to. This prevents test systems from accidentally sending emails to real external recipients.

If a recipient email address is correctly configured but the domain is not on the allowed list for that test system, the output will fail with this error.

2.1 How to Configure Allowed Receiver Domains (Test Systems)

  1. Open the configure activity “Define Email Receiver Addresses for Test Systems (ID: 101312)
  2. Add the allowed receiver domain(s). Entries must start with an asterisk (*). For example:

*@company.com          (allows all addresses in the company.com domain)
*@partner.com          (allows all addresses in the partner.com domain)

Note: This setting applies to test systems only and is not required in production systems. In production, the system can send to any valid email address without this restriction.

Error 3: "Sender Domain in xxxx@xxxx.com Is Not Allowed" / "Domain in Sender Email Address Is Not Allowed"

This error occurs when the sender email address used in Output Parameter Determination contains a domain that has not been registered as an allowed sender domain in the system configuration. This is a security control to prevent SAP from sending emails from domains the customer does not own or has not authorised.

This error can appear in two scenarios:

  • At output runtime: when an output item is processed and the sender domain has not been configured.
  • At OPD configuration time: when a user tries to define a Sender Email in Output Parameter Determination and the domain of that address is not registered.

3.1 How to Configure Allowed Sender Domains

  1. Open the configuration activity “Define Email Sender Domain (ID: 101313)”.
  2. Enter the domain(s) that are allowed as sender domains. The following formats are supported:

Sophia.Tester@company.com (allows emails sent to this email address)
*@company.com (allows all addresses with the domain company.com)
*.company.com (allows all email addresses in the subdomain company.com, e.g. Sophia@sales.company.com)

Warning: Do NOT include the @ character when entering a domain. For example, enter company.com, not @company.com. Including @ will cause the configuration to fail silently.

Warning: The SAP domain @sap.com must NOT be used in customer systems. Since SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2108, checks were introduced specifically to block the usage of SAP domains (sap.com or @sap.com) in the sender domain configuration.

Note: You can add multiple domains if your organisation sends from more than one domain (e.g., different domains per company code or region).

3.2 Also Define a Default Sender Email Address

As a best practice, also configure a default sender email address. This is used whenever no specific sender address is determined by the output logic. To configure it:

  1. Open the configuration activity “Define a Default Email Sender Address (ID: 101311)”.
  2. Enter a valid sender address from your registered domain, for example:

do.not.reply@company.com

Error 4: "Sender Email Address Can't Be Initial"

This error appears when the system cannot find any sender email address for the output. It is most commonly seen in payment advice processing through the Manage Automatic Payments app (App ID: F0770), but can also occur in other document types.

The error means that the Sender Email field in Output Parameter Determination for the relevant output type is either blank or has not been configured at all.

4.1 Fix: Maintain Sender Email in OPD for Payment Advice

  1. Open the Output Parameter Determination app.
  2. Set Show Rules for to, e.g.: Payment Advice.
  3. Set Determination Step to, e.g.: Email Settings.
  4. Choose the Edit button.
  5. Under Table Contents, choose + (Insert New Row) and fill in the following, e.g.:

Output type:      PAYM_ADV
Sender Email:     <your sender email address>
Email Template:   FFO_PAYM_ADVICE_EMAIL_TEMPLATE

  1. Save the configuration.

Note: The email template FFO_PAYM_ADVICE_EMAIL_TEMPLATE is delivered by SAP as a standard template. You can copy and customize it to adapt the subject line and email body to your business requirements.

Tip: Make sure the sender email domain is also registered in the allowed sender domains. Using an email address whose domain is not registered will result in the 'sender domain not allowed' error even after you fill in the sender email.

Quick Reference: Error Summary and Where to Fix

Error Message

Root Cause

Where to Fix

No email recipient has been maintained

Recipient email not configured in OPD or business partner master data

OPD (Email Recipient step) and/or Maintain Business Partner

Recipient address x.y@abc.com not allowed

Receiver domain not whitelisted on test/quality system

Config Activity ID 101312

Sender domain in xxxx@xxxx.com isn't allowed / Domain in sender email address is not allowed

Sender domain not registered as an allowed domain

Config Activity ID 101313 & 101311

Sender email address can't be initial

Sender email not configured in OPD for the output type

OPD > Show Rules for: &1 > Determination Step: Email Settings > Output type

entry.title: The Quiet Revolution in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud Logistics: Clean Core, Transparency, and AI
entry.content:
Supply chains are no longer just about moving physical goods; they are highly synchronized, data-driven ecosystems. As we navigate the latest updates in SAP S/4HANA—particularly with the "progress without disruption" philosophy of the 2025 releases—it is clear that the focus has shifted. We are moving away from reactive workarounds and toward proactive, intelligent, and strictly governed logistics.

For those of us working deep in the trenches of Sourcing, Procurement, and Warehouse Management, this shift brings both exciting capabilities and new operational challenges. Here is a look at the three pillars redefining our day-to-day logistics architecture in the Public Cloud.

1. The Discipline of Clean Core Extensibility

Historically, enforcing specific business rules—like making the "Requisitioner" or "Purchasing Organization" fields mandatory on a Purchase Requisition—often led to messy backend modifications. Today, the Public Cloud enforces a much-needed discipline.

Standard customizing has its limits, but leveraging Key User Extensibility is proving to be a game-changer. By utilizing specific Custom Logic (BAdIs) for Field Control (MM_PUR_S4_PR_FLDCNTRL_SIMPLE) and Item Checks (MM_PUR_S4_PR_CHECK), we can enforce strict data quality right at the entry point of procurement. This approach ensures compliance without compromising the clean core, guaranteeing that our systems remain upgrade-safe and resilient.

2. The Push for Total Process Transparency

True efficiency requires total visibility, yet there are still standard gaps we need to address as a community. A perfect example is workflow simulation.

With release 2602, SAP introduced the highly anticipated ability to simulate workflows for Purchase Orders, allowing teams to validate conditions before documents are processed. However, this same transparency is critically missing for Purchase Requisitions. PRs are the genesis of the procurement lifecycle. Without simulation capabilities at the PR level, organizations are left relying on reactive troubleshooting and trial-and-error. Pushing for these types of intuitive enhancements (like supporting IR #365219) is vital for maintaining process consistency from end to end.

3. Smarter Warehousing and System Resilience

Execution is where strategy meets reality. In the Warehouse Management space, the transition to Public Cloud highlights the need for advanced, automated housekeeping. While traditional EWM robustly handles rearrangement and slotting, Public Cloud WM environments still require creative approaches or scheduled optimization jobs to handle routine bin cleanups and stock consolidation effectively.

As we look toward the future of smart logistics—where predictive maintenance data and real-time equipment health will directly trigger warehouse and procurement tasks—backend stability is non-negotiable. SAP’s recent moves, such as introducing Multi-Availability Zones for the BTP ABAP environment and offering scalable 0.5 ACU runtimes for agile development, provide the exact infrastructure needed to build these high-volume, highly resilient integrations.

Bridging the Gap with AI

Finally, the tools to build this future are evolving faster than ever. From leveraging AI-powered UI5 plugins for rapid application development to integrating Joule into the end-user experience, the technology is ready. The remaining barrier is no longer software; it is the skills gap. As an ecosystem, our next major hurdle is moving past the hype and focusing on the tactical, day-one skills required to actually deploy these AI tools in live logistics environments.

What are your thoughts? Are you finding that Clean Core extensibility is fully meeting your complex procurement needs, or are there still gaps in standard Public Cloud WM functionality that your teams are struggling to work around? Let’s discuss in the comments below!

entry.title: Unlocking the Power of AI‑Assisted Node.js Development with VS Code, Cline, and SAP AI Core
entry.content:
AI‑assisted development is transforming how developers build, test, and optimize applications. Gone are the days of switching endlessly between documentation, terminals, and dashboards. Today, intelligent coding tools like Cline, integrated directly into VS Code, work alongside developers — understanding context, planning steps, and even writing code interactively.
When connected with SAP AI Core, this experience is elevated to an enterprise‑grade level: you can harness SAP’s generative AI capabilities right inside your Node.js projects without leaving your IDE. In this blog, we’ll walk through a complete setup and workflow to unleash this power step by step.   
 
🧩 Step 1: Set Up Your Prerequisites
Before we dive in, make sure you have:
  • VS Code (latest version)
  • Node.js 18+ installed
  • SAP BTP account with AI Core access (extended plan recommended)
  • Cline extension for VS Code
🧩 Step 2 : Installing Cline
  • Open VS Code.
  • Go to the Extensions tab (Ctrl+Shift+X or Cmd+Shift+X).
  • Search for “Cline”.
  • Install the official extension (no additional fork needed).
  • Restart VS Code.

 
🧩 Step 3 : Connect Cline to SAP AI Core
SAP AI Core gives Cline access to enterprise‑grade generative models in your SAP BTP environment. Integration is simple.
Get Your Service Key
  1. In your SAP BTP Cockpit, go to your subaccount.
  2. Under Services → Service Marketplace, find SAP AI Core.
  3. Create an instance (plan: extended).
  4. Generate a Service Key and copy the following information:
    • Client ID
    • Client Secret
    • Auth URL
    • Base URL
    • Resource Group (usually "default")
Please refer the steps as mentioned https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/ai-core-genaihub-provisioning..html=
Step 4: Configure Cline In VS Code

 

In VS Code, open Cline → Settings → API Configuration, and fill in:

 
Cline Setting SAP AI Core Value
AI Core Client Idclientid
AI Core Client Secretclientsecret
AI Core Base URLserviceurls/AI_API_URL
AI Core Auth URLauth_url
AI Core Resource Group"default"

Once saved, Cline will connect securely to your SAP AI Core environment. You're now running on SAP’s Generative AI Hub infrastructure.

 

Step 5: Verify That Cline Is Working
  1. Click the Cline icon in the VS Code sidebar
  2. Type a test prompt, such as: “Hi”
  3. Press Enter
💡Step 6: Start a Node.js Project with AI

Now it’s time to let Cline assist you from the beginning.

 Project Setup Instruction
## Step 1: Create a  project 
Create an empty Nodejs CAP project 
this creates 
 

 

## Step 2: Environment Setup
Create a environment  file and set the port to 4000
## Step 3: Express Server Setup
Create a file to load the env variables, and start express on that port. Add a simple /health route and add a GET /employees route that uses axios to fetch users from "https://jsonplaceholder.typicode.com/users" and return only their name and email

## Step 4: CAP Data Model

 

Create a entity called Employees that has ID as Integer, name as String, and email as String
## Step 5: CAP Service Definition
Create a service to expose the Employees entity as a projection, and add an unbound action called fetchTodos that takes an employeeId Integer and returns a String array
this creates
## Step 6: Override Employees READ Operation

 

Override the READ of Employees. Do NOT use a database. Instead, use axios to GET https://jsonplaceholder.typicode.com/users, map the results to match the CDS entity fields, and return the array
 ## Step 7: Implement fetchTodos Action

 

Implement the fetchTodos action in the same file. Use axios to get todos from https://jsonplaceholder.typicode.com/users/:employeeId/todos and return an array of just the todo titles

## Step 8: Run Locally

You can either use Cline to run and scaffold the project, or directly execute the standard CAP command like cds watch to start the application and test the services.

🎯Conclusion

Combining VS Code, Cline, and SAP AI Core brings a new era of Node.js development — one where natural language drives coding, SAP‑grade AI enriches solutions, and productivity soars. From scaffolding APIs to integrating intelligent services, this workflow empowers developers to focus on creativity instead of configuration.

The fusion of agentic coding and SAP’s enterprise AI stack isn’t just productivity‑boosting — it’s redefining how SAP developers build the future.

Ready to start vibing?
💬Install Cline, connect to your SAP AI Core, and experience AI‑first Node.js development today.

entry.title: How to undeploy a Joule project in SAP Build environments

Step 1: Open the project and find the release version you have deployed in the environment.

Step 2: Click on Deploy and, select the enviroment and then click on Undeploy.

This step is important to be able to delete Projects and/or Actions.

Best regards,

Erick

entry.title: Re: The application 'Test your Processes' is not running.
entry.content:
Dear @Ryo_Yokoyama,

In Public Cloud this issue is usually caused by the Test Automation Tool setup, especially the DEFAULT user / role credentials rather than by the J58 process itself. SAP documents this exact symptom test plans in Test Your Processes can remain at 0%, not progress or stay in In Process status. SAP recommended checks are to validate the test user credentials, check the authentication/IDP setup, stop the stuck execution and then re-execute the test plan

More details below:

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3087418

The key checks are:

1. In Test Your Processes, open Maintain Roles and verify the user maintained for DEFAULT

2. Confirm that the DEFAULT user exists in the S/4HANA Cloud system is not locked or expired and has valid credentials

3. Log in manually with the same test user credentials to confirm that the password is valid

4. Re-maintain the DEFAULT credentials in Maintain Roles

More details below:

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3082337

5. Check that all test users/roles used by the process steps are valid and not locked, expired or maintained with an incorrect password. SAP confirms that issues with business users, roles or DEFAULT setup can stop the test plan execution from progressing

More details below:

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3557201

6. If SSO or an external Identity Provider is used, verify the login setup. SAP specifically mentions SSO / unsupported external IDP as a possible reason for the 0% issue in some cases the resolution is to use valid credentials instead of SSO for the test execution

More details below:

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/2820953

7. Stop the stuck execution and run the test plan again after correcting the setup. SAP also lists insufficient execution resources as a possible cause and recommends stopping the ongoing execution and re-executing the test plan

Best Regards,

Dawid
