entry.title: Automatic batch determination with releasing the production order
entry.content:
Hi all,

I am working on S/4HANA Cloud, Public Edition and trying to set up automatic batch determination for components when a production order is released.

In the material master (MRP 2 view), the field 'Batch Entry' only offers value 3 – 'Batch Determination at Order Release', but not value 4, which  triggers the batch determination fully automatically in the background during order release.

With value 3, it seems the system only checks whether a batch determination is expected, but does not execute it automatically – meaning the user still has to manually click the 'Batch Determination' button in the component list of the production order.

My questions:
1. Is value 4 intentionally not available in S/4HANA Cloud, Public Edition (Clean Core / reduced scope)?
2. Is there any standard way to trigger batch determination fully automatically upon production order release in the Public Cloud, without manual user interaction?
3. Or is the recommended SAP Best Practice in the Public Cloud to perform batch determination at the time of goods issue rather than at order release?

Any experience or guidance from the community would be greatly appreciated!

Thanks in advance.

Jana

entry.title: SAP Inside Track Sydney 2026 | Friday September 4, 9:00am - 6pm
entry.content:
SAP Inside Track Sydney 2026

A reimagined SAP community event focused on real-world insights, collaboration, and learning.


Event Details

Date: Friday, September 4, 2026 | 9am to 6pm
Location: The Mint, 10 Macquarie Street, Sydney
Format: Full-day, in-person event


Registration

Registration information will be published soon.

Attention: Registration for the event will take place using a separate event platform and not via the attendance RSVP section on the right-hand side.

Please follow this page for updates on attendee registration, agenda announcements, speaker confirmations, and other event news.

About the Event

SAP Inside Track Sydney 2026 brings the SAP Community together for a full day of learning, connection, and practical insight.

This year’s event is a refreshed take on the traditional SAP Inside Track format, with an elevated venue experience, a sharper agenda, and a strong focus on content that is relevant to today’s business environment and SAP landscape.

Expect a day of presentations, demos, hands-on labs, shaped by knowledge sharing, community spirit, and relevant discussion across the key topics influencing SAP customers and practitioners today.

Whether you are interested in enterprise architecture, SAP strategy, development, automation, data and AI, or customer experience, SAP Inside Track Sydney 2026 is designed to create valuable opportunities to learn from others, exchange ideas, and strengthen connections across the local SAP ecosystem.


Why Attend

  • Hear practical perspectives from SAP customers, partners, and community speakers
  • Explore key topics across architecture, development, automation, data and AI, strategy, and product updates
  • Connect with peers from across the SAP ecosystem
  • Experience a refreshed SAP Inside Track in a standout Sydney CBD venue


Venue

SAP Inside Track Sydney 2026 will be held at The Mint, one of Sydney’s most historic and distinctive venues.

Its heritage character, combined with a striking modern auditorium and courtyard surrounds, creates a unique backdrop for a reimagined SAP community event.

Located on Macquarie Street in the Sydney CBD, The Mint is conveniently within walking distance of Martin Place, Town Hall, and Wynyard.

The Mint, Sydney


Content Themes and Agenda

The agenda will feature a mix of presentations and demos designed to combine strategic insight with practical, real-world experience.

Planned content themes include:

  • Enterprise Architecture
  • Customer Stories
  • Development
  • Automation
  • Data and AI
  • SAP Strategy
  • Product Roadmaps and Updates

A draft agenda format is provided below.  More details on the agenda will be shared as the event planning progresses. 
Additionally, there will be breakout streams for hands-on labs and demos.

TimeSession
09:00am - 09:25amRegistration
09:30am - 09:50amOpening Remarks
09:55am - 10:25am Session 1
10:30am - 11:00amSession 2
11:05am - 11:25amMorning Tea
11:30am - 12:00pmSession 3
12:05pm - 12:35pmSession 4
12:35pm - 01:35pmLunch
01:40pm - 02:10pmSession 5: Panel Discussion
02:15pm - 02:45pmSession 6
02:50pm - 03:10pmAfternoon Tea
03:15pm - 04:15pmSession 7
04:20pm - 04:50pmSession 8
04:50pm - 05:00pmWrap-up and SAP Press book draw
05:00pm - 06:00pmNetworking Drinks

 

Call for Speakers

Details on the call for speakers process will be announced soon.

entry.title: Re: Email notification to purhaser on award of quotation...
entry.content:
Dear @Talha_Naeem-071,

No, this is not available as a standard Public Cloud configuration without workflow. If Flexible Workflow is not to be used Public Cloud does not provide a standard setting that sends an email to the purchaser exactly when a supplier quotation is awarded against an RFQ

Possible approach without Flexible Workflow:

1. Use SAP BTP / SAP Integration Suite as the extension layer

2. Detect the award indirectly by using released integration content:

    • Supplier Quotation API/service to read supplier quotation data. SAP documents the service for reading, modifying, deleting and creating supplier quotations with reference to an RFQ

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/bb9f1469daf04bd894ab2167f8132a1a/72953820cc7c47fc9cdacd9702decf4d.html

    • RFQ business events where applicable. SAP documents RFQ events including an event raised when a follow-on document is created

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/bb9f1469daf04bd894ab2167f8132a1a/11992e4361e64e8b875d06705d73434c.html

3. Check whether the quotation / RFQ has reached the relevant awarded or follow-on-document-created state

4. Determine the purchaser from the RFQ / purchasing group / internal responsibility mapping

5. Send the email from the extension layer

6. Store a notification log so the same award does not trigger duplicate emails 

SAP supports event-based extensions in Public Cloud through Business Event Handling / Event Mesh but this requires the relevant event topic to be available and activated for the tenant. SAP documents that events from Public Cloud can be published and consumed by BTP applications and that Business Event Handling scope item 1NN plus SAP Event Mesh are part of this setup

More details below:

https://learning.sap.com/courses/implementing-sap-s-4hana-cloud-public-edition/setting-up-customer-driven-integrations

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0f69f8fb28ac4bf48d2b57b9637e81fa/8cbf952e55364254be2da77aa1342aa5.html

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: Re: Add custom field in the Project Control - Project Bri...
entry.content:
With the "Custom Fields" app, you can create and maintain custom fields that you can use to enhance applications.

You can create custom fields using the business context 'Project Element', enable usage of the custom field for the "Project Briefs" app in the User Interfaces section of custom field maintenance, and publish your custom fields. 

Please more details in the SAP Help Portal: App Extensibility: Project Briefs | SAP Help Portal

entry.title: 在SAP S/4HANA Cloud 公有云生产系统中更改会计年度变式
entry.content:
真实世界的业务是动态的。合并、收购、售卖公司或新的战略报表要求可能意味着您现有的会计年度不再满足变化的需要。

您可能会在以下情况中找到和您相同的处境：

* 场景 1：需要调整会计年度：贵公司一直在日历年（1 月 - 12 月）运营，但由于最近收购，您现在需要与会计年度从 7 月开始的新母公司保持一致。这需要创建“缩短的会计年度”，以弥合从 12 月到 7 月新会计年度开始的差距。

* 场景 2：重新定义期间：您将从标准日历月移动到零售日历，如 4-4-5 周结构。这从根本上改变了全年的过账期间定义方式。

如果您已经进入系统配置尝试进行更改，则可能会发现会计年度变式 (FYV) 字段在生产环境中不可编辑。我想解释为什么会这样，更重要的是，引导您找到正确的解决方案。

挑战：为什么我不能改动呢？

会计年度定义是财务系统中最基本的配置之一。基本上从统一日记账 (ACDOCA) 中的日记账分录到计划数据 (ACDOCP) 和资产价值的每个财务交易都与会计年度和过账期间相关联。

在没有结构化方法的情况下，在生产系统中回溯更改此定义，就像在您仍然生活在房屋中时尝试更改房屋的承重结构。这可能会导致严重的数据不一致和错误报告。由于此复杂性和跨不同应用范围的深层相关性，因此在生产系统中不允许此更改。

解决方案：由专家主导的专门项目

对于 SAP S/4HANA Cloud（公共版），生产系统中会计年度定义的更改将作为 SAP Data Management and Landscape Transformation (DMLT) 咨询服务提供的客户个人咨询项目进行处理。

这不仅仅是一个简单的配置切换；它是一种全面的服务，包括：

* 分析：专家将与您一起了解所需变更的全部范围和影响。

* 配置：将仔细配置新的会计年度变式。

* 数据转换：如有必要，将转换主数据和事务数据以与新结构保持一致。

* 支持：该团队将为您提供客户特定步骤的支持，以确保所有相关任务和应用程序的平稳过渡。

我们强烈建议您提前对此进行计划。在流程早期与审计员互动，并为项目留出足够的提前期。

如何开始

准备好采取下一步行动了吗？以下是官方流程：

1.发起请求：要正式请求更改会计年度定义，请使用组件 CA-LT-SRV-2CL 创建案例。

2.早期参与：如前所述，您联系 SAP DMLT 咨询服务越早，我们为您准备和计划服务就越好。

需要牢记的重要注意事项

为确保您清楚了解，以下是有关服务的几个要点：

* 系统前提条件：此服务支持通过 SAP Central Business Configuration (CBC) 管理的 SAP S/4HANA Cloud（公共版）实施。

* 范围：目前，该服务涵盖跨所有分类账和公司代码分配的单个唯一会计年度变式的变更。

* 新变式标识：如果您当前正在使用预配置的 SAP 会计年度变式（例如 K4），则项目将需要创建新的客户特定变式标识（例如 Z4）。作为服务的一部分，包含旧变式标识（如表 ACDOCA 中的行项目）的数据将转换为新变式标识。

* 面向未来的变更：对会计年度定义的更改仅影响未来的数据和流程。历史交易数据的修改不在范围内。

*进一步参考 3678328 - Change of Fiscal Year Variant in S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

您的财务框架至关重要，应精准、谨慎地根据不断变化的业务需求调整财务框架。虽然您无法通过单击开关简单的进行此更改，但可以使用结构化、专家主导的路径来确保其正确完成。

entry.title: Re: Howdo we handle Q stock posting through QM management...
entry.content:
Thank you for your query.

Based on my understanding, QM is active for the material and the stock is posted to Quality Inspection stock (Q‑stock), while there is a requirement to move the stock before a Usage Decision (UD) is made.

In SAP standard, if no UD has been taken, the only possible way to move Q‑stock is via T-code QAC2 (Stock Posting for Inspection Lot). These postings remain fully under QM governance and audit control.

To the best of my knowledge, SAP S/4HANA does not provide a standard “rolling” or partially released Q‑stock concept that allows unrestricted or standard logistics movements while keeping the stock under QM control. This would contradict the core QM design principle, where Q‑stock represents a strong logistical and quality lock. In this respect, the behavior of Quality Inspection stock in S/4HANA standard QM is the same as in ECC.

In the interim, although the custom current date may be displayed as "System Date" within the range filter panel, our internal assessments suggest that the date range filter is correctly applied to the custom widget, effectively filtering data according to the specified custom current date range. entry.title: Re: How to capture all the existing Fiori catalogs and ti...
entry.content:
Hello,

Please consider the following steps:

Capturing Fiori Catalogs & Tiles in a Single TR
Step 1: Create a TR
SE09 → Create → Workbench/Customizing Request
Note down TR number (e.g., DEV#K900123)

Step 2: Mass Transport of All Catalogs & Tiles
SE38 → Run Program: /UI2/FLPCM_TRANSPORT_ALL
Select: Catalogs, Tiles, Groups/Spaces, Pages
Assign your TR number → Execute (F8)

Step 3: Transport Role-Based Tiles (PFCG)
PFCG → Open each Role → Menu Tab → Transport
Assign to the SAME TR number

Step 4: Verify TR Contents
SE09 → Open TR → Verify objects:
- FCLC = Catalog
- FCLT = Tile
- FGRP = Group
- AGR_HIERT = Role Menu

Step 5: Release & Transport
SE09 → Release Task → Release TR → STMS (Import to target)

Best regards,

Joe

entry.title: Error during Mass Maintenance: "Business Partner with GUID ... already exists"
entry.content:
Hi,

I am currently working on mass-updating Business Partners (BP) using the "Manage Mass Maintenance" app to set them to inactive status.

Context:

  1. I exported the current BP master data using "Export Master Data - Business Partners".

  2. In the file, I mapped the following columns: SOURCE_ID, PARTNER, XBLCK, NAME_ORG1, NAME_ORG2, NAME_ORG3, VALID_FROM, and VALID_TO.

  3. To deactivate the BPs, I set the XBLCK field to 'X' and uploaded the file back into the "Manage Mass Maintenance - Business Partner" process.

Issue:
Instead of updating the existing records, the system returns the following error for many BPs:

"Business Partner with GUID [GUID_VALUE] already exists"

It seems the system is trying to create a new BP record instead of performing an update/change on the existing one, despite the PARTNER number being included in the input file.

Questions:

  1. Why does the system treat an update request as an attempt to create a duplicate record with the same GUID?

  2. Is there a specific configuration or an additional field (e.g., action code or specific indicator) required to explicitly instruct the system to "Update" rather than "Create"?

  3. What are the mandatory columns and their specific values required to perform an "Inactive" status update for existing BPs in the "Manage Mass Maintenance" app? (For example, is XBLCK alone sufficient, or do I need to include specific validity dates or action indicators?)

Any guidance on the correct procedure for mass-deactivating existing BPs via this app would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you.
